blocks.text: But you, our God, are kind and true,
For even if we sin we are yours, knowing your power;
For to know you is complete righteousness,
For neither has the evil intent of human art misled us,
whose appearance arouses yearning in fools,
Lovers of evil things and fit for such objects of hope
A potter kneads the soft earth
With misspent toil, these workers form a futile god from the same clay—
But the workers are not concerned that mortals are destined to die
Their heart is ashes, their hope is cheaper than dirt,
because they failed to know the one who formed them
But they considered our existence an idle game
For these persons, more than all others, know that they sin
But most foolish and more miserable than an infant
For they thought that all the idols of the nations were gods,
People are mortal, and what they make with lawless hands is dead,
Moreover, they worship even the most hateful animals,
and even as animals they are not so beautiful in appearance that one would desire them,
